findings.description: mask_middle only fully masks when len(value) <= visible*2, so a value just one char longer (e.g. 9 chars with visible=4) leaks 8 of 9 characters, contradicting the docstring's intent to avoid leaking most of the content.
mask_middle does not validate that mask_char is a single character; a multi-char mask_char inflates output length and breaks the implied fixed-width masking contract.
indent_block uses splitlines() which drops a trailing newline and treats whitespace-only lines as empty, so text is not round-tripped and 'non-empty line' semantics are subtly violated.
slugify does not validate max_length, so a negative value makes slug[:max_length] silently drop trailing characters instead of truncating from the front.
pluralize uses abs(count)==1 so a count of -1 yields the singular noun ('-1 item'), which is debatable English and may surprise callers.
truncate counts by code points, so a multi-codepoint suffix (e.g. an emoji with combining marks) could miscount visible width even though the character-length invariant holds.
slugify drops all non-ASCII via NFKD+ascii-ignore, silently discarding transliteratable characters (e.g. accented letters lose their base form differently than callers might expect for some scripts).
indent_block materializes the full list of split lines and a new joined string; for very large inputs this doubles memory versus a streaming approach.
Module-level functions lack type-guard handling for non-str inputs despite annotations, so passing None/bytes raises low-level AttributeErrors rather than clear contract errors.
